2.gl: gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a superlative
2.kj: angels, [idiom] exceeding, God (gods) (-dess, -ly), [idiom] (very) great, judges, [idiom] mighty.

2.gl: properly, like this, i.e. by implication, (of manner) thus (or so); also (of place) here (or hither); or (of time) now

2.gl: Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
2.kj: Jehovah, the Lord. Compare H3050 (יָהּ), H3069 (יְהֹוִה).

2.gl: Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also (typically) of his posterity

3.dv: a primitive particle of incitement and entreaty, which may usually be rendered;

3.gl: repose or (adverbially) peacefully; figuratively, consolation (specifically, matrimony); hence (concretely) an abode
3.kj: comfortable, ease, quiet, rest(-ing place), still.

3.gl: Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
